t: Improvement with treatment; risk of progression to stable or full PD.
Myth 1: "Personality Difficulty is not a real diagnosis"
ICD-11 is the official code; it provides clinical attention to subclinical personality problems.
Myth 2: "Personality difficulty inevitably turns into PD"
Some patients experience deterioration, but most stabilize or improve.
Myth 3: "Should only be treated with medication"
Psychotherapy is first-line; pharmacotherapy for comorbid symptoms.
Myth 4: "This category is only for labeling 'anxious' patients"
ICD-11 — clinical response to functional difficulty; provides clinical support.
